logline: A systems engineer keeps a deprecated AI companion alive for the elderly users who still depend on it, long after the world has moved on.
scene: a systems engineer at a cluttered home desk late at night, a single old server humming on a shelf beside her, her face lit by a terminal showing system logs, the rest of the apartment dark, a mug of cold tea beside her, the intimate scale of maintaining something small and forgotten, cool blue monitor light with warm desk lamp
keyProps: A single old server humming on a shelf
Dark apartment with isolated pools of light
camera: Intimate workspace framing, 35mm lens, low-key lighting emphasizing isolation
lighting: Cool blue monitor light as primary illumination with a warm desk lamp creating a small bubble of light, the rest of the apartment in darkness
productionDesign: Cool desaturated palette, 35mm film look, cluttered home workspace with aging technology, melancholic atmosphere of quiet dedication to something forgotten
signatureTwist: close-up of an elderly man's hands holding a simple tablet device showing a basic conversation interface, the hands weathered and dark-skinned, warm morning light from a window casting golden streaks across the device, the interface simple and old-fashioned compared to modern designs, the man's thumbs poised to type, care home setting out of focus
